title: practical english usage - Part 193
content:
There's some steak in the fridge if you're hungry.

'I’ m  saying this in case .. .' If-clauses are quite often used to explain the purpose of a remark - to suggest 12 if  meaning       'I'm saying     this   in  case' grammar •  247  unless the-car breaks down-soon-.) not I'll be surprised if the car doesn't break down soon, (     I'll be surprised unless breaks down.) - I'll drive over and see you, unless the car breaks down. (= ... except if the car late it wm be the first time this week. The  meaning is not ’ except if.) not If my train isn’ t late it will be the first time this week, (     Unless my train's my  train’ s late.) - OK. So we'll meet this evening at 7.00 - unless my train's late. (= ... except if Compare: Unless means ’ except if; it is not used with other meanings of ’ if... not’ .

2 when     unless       cannot      be   used I'm going to dig the garden this afternoon, unless it rains.

Let's have dinner out - unless you're too tired.

I'll be back tomorrow unless there's a plane strike.

except if the pay is too low.) I'll take the job unless the pay is too low. (= ... if the pay isn’ t too low / Come tomorrow unless I phone. (= ... if I don’ t phone / except if 1 phone.) Unless has a similar meaning to if... not, in the sense of ’ except if.

For suggestions with suppose, supposing and uihat if, ►    599.

You're welcome to stay with us as/so long as you share the expenses.

I'll give you the day off on condition that you work on Saturday morning.

You can borrow my bike providing/provided you bring it back.

Supposing you'd missed the train. What would you have done?

Imagine we could all fly. Wouldn't that be fun!

provided (that), as/so long as, on condition (that) (used to make conditions).

supposing (that) (used to talk about what might happen), and providing (that), similar structures. Some of the commonest are imagine (that), suppose (that), Many words and expressions can be used in similar ways to if, and often with simila£uses                __ 246 other     words     and   expressions         with If I didn't have my walking boots on, I think I would have really hurt my foot.

If I had the money with me, I would have bought you one.

If I knew you were coming I'd have baked a cake.

normal. This is more common in American English.

Sometimes a simple past tense is used with if where a past perfect would be 4 mixed     tenses If   Section 22 grammar •  248  in case and if In case of fire, break glass. (= If there is a fire ...) British English.

in case, and can be used in similar situations to /ʃin both American English and The  prepositional phrase in case of has a wider meaning than the conjunction 4 in   case     of In case you're free this evening give me a call, (less typical of British English) In American English, in case is often used in a similar way to if.

(    . .. telephone. . . in ease their houses catch fire.) People telephone the fire brigade if their houses catch fire.

- People insure their houses in case they catch fire, (     .. .if they each fire.) noi I'll open the umbrella if it rains, (     I 'll open the umbrella in case it rains.) - I'm taking an umbrella in case it rains.

comes, then we ’ ll buy the wine. If he doesn’ t we  won't.) Let's buy a bottle of wine if George comes. (= We  ’ ll wait and see. If George because George might come  later.) - Let's buy a bottle of wine in case George comes. (= Let’ s buy some wine now 'Do A  if B happens’  means 'Do A  if B has already happened’ . Compare: 'Do A  in case B happens’  means 'Do A  (first) because B might happen later'.

In British English, in case and if are normally used in quite different ways.

3 in   case     and   if ... in case your mother should stay to lunch.

very common.

Should can be used in the same way in British English, but this is no longer We took our swimming things in case we happened to find a pool.

I've bought a chicken in case your mother happens to stay to lunch.

'by chance’ .

We   can use the expression happen to after in case to emphasise the meaning of 2 in   case     ...  happen        to mother will stay, ..) not I've bought a chicken in case your mother stays to lunch, (     . . .in case your To  talk about the future, we  use a present tense after in case (►  231).
